forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCBDXJKQA68WPEE4MJW77ZH
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '9780'
+title: Migrate sah dynamic_cli.rs to shared generator for per-op precise args
+---
+## What
+Replace sah's imprecise global-union arg logic in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/dynamic_cli.rs` with the shared schema-driven generator (card B), so each verb advertises only its own operation's params with correct required flags.
+
+The bug today: `create_command_data_from_tool` (`dynamic_cli.rs:1096`) reads `tool.operations()` (:1097) but computes args ONCE from the global `properties` block via `precompute_args(schema)` (:1103) and clones that full union onto every verb in `create_verb_command_data` (:1163, the clone at :1168). Result: every verb (e.g. `kanban task move`) advertises ALL kanban params and the required flags are wrong.
+
+Approach: have sah build commands from each tool's FULL schema using `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen::build_commands_from_schema`, and extract args with `::extract_noun_verb_arguments`, instead of the bespoke `create_noun_command_data`/`create_verb_command_data`/`precompute_args` union path. sah's command layer wraps clap in its own `CommandData`/`ArgData` structs — pick the lower-risk seam:
+- Preferred: feed the per-op scoped args (from the shared generator's per-op `x-operation-schemas` read) into `CommandData`, replacing the global `schema_args` clone at :1168, so the rest of sah's dynamic-CLI plumbing is unchanged.
+- Obtain the full schema in-process from `tool.operations()` via the `_full` generator (card C/D), NOT from the wire `schema()` (which is slim after card D).
+
+Trace and preserve the dispatch side: wherever sah turns matched args back into the tool-call JSON, ensure it produces the same `{ "op": "verb noun", ...scoped args }` object the tools' forgiving `parse_input` expects.
+
+Files: `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/dynamic_cli.rs` (primary), plus any arg-extraction call site this feeds.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Each verb subcommand under a tool advertises ONLY its operation's params (per-op scoping), with that op's required fields enforced.
+- [ ] `sah kanban task move` rejects invocations missing that op's required params and does NOT accept params belonging to other ops.
+- [ ] The global-union path (`precompute_args` cloned onto every verb at :1168) is removed; full workspace builds.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Add/extend tests in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli` (alongside existing `dynamic_cli` tests): assert that for a representative op (`move task`) the generated verb command's arg set equals that op's params (not the union), and that a required param is flagged required while a non-member param is absent.
+- [ ] A negative test: parsing `kanban task move` without its required field errors; parsing with a foreign param errors (unknown arg).
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-cli dynamic_cli` passes.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the per-op precision + required-enforcement tests first (they fail against today's union path), then swap in the shared generator.
\ No newline at end of file

+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-07 15:58)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:62` — The entire CLI lifecycle scaffolding — build_cli plus serve/init/deinit/doctor/skill/completion subcommand builders — is reimplemented here as a near-verbatim copy of the same functions in shelltool-cli and kanban-cli. This is the third copy; a bug or help-text fix now has to be made in three places and they will drift. The codebase already extracts shared CLI machinery into crates (swissarmyhammer_cli_completions, swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen), so there is an established home for this. Extract a shared lifecycle-CLI builder (e.g. in swissarmyhammer_operations or a small swissarmyhammer-cli-lifecycle module) parameterized on binary name / about string / schema, and have code-context, shelltool, and kanman call it. The per-binary differences are pure data (name, about, op schema), not contract differences.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:191` — install_target_arg, target_value, and target_to_scope are byte-identical to the copies in kanban-cli and shelltool-cli — the install-target vocabulary (project/local/user) and its mapping to InitScope is now defined three times. A new scope or a renamed value silently diverges across binaries. Reuse one canonical helper for the target arg + target→InitScope mapping. The existing `InitScope::from(InstallTarget)` pattern in avp-cli (install.rs:17) is the natural shared form; lift it so all four CLIs share it instead of re-stringly-typing the scope list.
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:320` — run_completion is a near-verbatim third copy; it also overlaps the completion-command logic already centralized in swissarmyhammer-cli's CliBuilder. Completion dispatch should have one implementation, not one per binary. Fold run_completion into the shared lifecycle module from finding 1 (it only needs the rebuilt Command + shell), so it rides along with the same extraction.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:95` — The lifecycle subcommand builders (`serve_subcommand`, `init_subcommand`, `deinit_subcommand`, `doctor_subcommand`) are near-identical across all three CLIs, differing only in the `about` text and install-target help string. This is a near-match that should be parameterized, not copied a third time. Provide shared builders parameterized on the varying strings, e.g. `serve_subcommand(about: &str)`, `lifecycle_subcommands(names: …)`, in the shared CLI module. Each CLI passes its own `about`/help text instead of re-declaring the clap scaffolding.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:162` — `install_target_arg` is a byte-for-byte copy of the same helper already in two other CLIs. Three identical copies of the project/local/user target arg means a bug or value-set change must now be made in three places instead of one. Hoist a single `install_target_arg(help: &'static str) -> clap::Arg` into a shared module — the natural home is `swissarmyhammer_operations::cli_gen`, which already owns `build_commands_from_schema`/`extract_noun_verb_arguments` that all three CLIs consume. Have all three call it.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:170` — `target_value` is identical to the same accessor in the other two CLIs — a third verbatim copy of `matches.get_one::<String>(\"target\")…unwrap_or(\"project\")`. Extract alongside `install_target_arg` in the shared `cli_gen` module; the two are a matched pair (arg definition + accessor) and should travel together.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:300` — `completion_subcommand` + `run_completion` duplicate the completion-command scaffolding that already exists in both sibling CLIs and in `swissarmyhammer-cli`'s `dynamic_cli`. The long_about block and the shell-extraction logic are copied with only the binary name changing. Extract a `completion_subcommand(bin: &str)` builder and a `run_completion` shared with the existing `swissarmyhammer_cli_completions` crate (which `completions::print_completion` already shims to). The four-way duplication — including `dynamic_cli` — makes a shared completion builder clearly warranted.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:444` — `dispatch_init_local_runs_registry` asserts `exit_code == 0 || exit_code == 1`, but `run_init` only ever returns 0 or 1, so the assertion spans its entire return domain and can never fail — the value check is tautological and verifies nothing about the dispatch result (it only proves dispatch didn't panic). Assert the observable side effect instead of the (host-variable) code, e.g. that `init local` created `.claude/settings.local.json` / `.shell/` under the tempdir — the doc comment already names these artifacts.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:461` — `dispatch_deinit_local_runs_registry` asserts `exit_code == 0 || exit_code == 1`, but `run_deinit` only ever returns 0 or 1, so the assertion can never fail and tests nothing about the result. Assert that `deinit local` removed the config artifact it is supposed to remove (e.g. the entry is gone from `.claude/settings.local.json`), rather than accepting any code in the function's full range.
+- [ ] `apps/shelltool-cli/src/main.rs:473` — `dispatch_doctor_runs_diagnostics` asserts `exit_code <= 2`, but `run_doctor`/`get_exit_code` only ever returns 0, 1, or 2, so the assertion spans the whole return domain and can never fail — it verifies nothing about the diagnostics outcome. Pin a deterministic outcome — e.g. assert the produced check set is non-empty / contains the expected check names — instead of a bound that every possible code already satisfies.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/board/get.rs:42` — GetBoard::execute is ~109 lines of actual code (body spans lines 43–168), far over the ~50-line guideline; it bundles board read, column/project sorting, the no-counts early-return shape, task enrichment, the per-column counting loop, column/project/tag mapping, and summary math into one function, making it hard to read and test each concern in isolation. Extract the distinct phases into helpers — e.g. a `basic_board_json(...)` for the no-counts branch (:62–77), a `count_tasks_by_column(&all_tasks) -> (column_counts, column_ready_counts, total_ready)` (:90–106), and a `build_summary(...)` (:135–161) — leaving execute as a short orchestration that reads, enriches, and assembles the response.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/main.rs:1` — `target_value` returns `.unwrap_or(\"project\")` while `install_target_arg` separately sets `.default_value(\"project\")`. The default for the same argument now lives in two places; if the arg default is later changed to e.g. \"local\", `target_value` silently disagrees. Two sources of truth for one default. Let the typed `EnumValueParser` + a `default_value_t` own the default (folds into the type-safety fix above), and drop the redundant `unwrap_or` fallback.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/schema.rs:475` — New test fn `serde_defaulted_fields_excluded_from_required_signatures` omits the `test_` prefix that every other test in this module uses, breaking the module's established naming pattern and making the test set inconsistent to scan/grep. Rename to `test_serde_defaulted_fields_excluded_from_required_signatures` to match the surrounding convention (mirrors the analogous `test_serde_default_field_not_required` test added in macro_expansion.rs).
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-operations-macros/src/lib.rs:130` — Required-detection only inspects field-level attributes, so a struct annotated with container-level #[serde(default)] (which makes every absent field fall back to Default, hence optional at dispatch) would still have its non-Option fields marked required. This is the same class of bug the change fixes, one level up — a schema-honoring CLI would reject valid no-arg invocations of such an operation. Pass the struct-level attrs into generate_param_meta (or compute a `container_has_default` flag once in `operation()` from `input.attrs`) and OR it into the `required` calculation: `let required = !is_option_type(&field.ty) && !has_serde_default(&field.attrs) && !container_default;`. At minimum, document the limitation on has_serde_default.
\ No newline at end of file

The field is then wrongly marked `required`, which is exactly the bug this function exists to prevent — and the swallowed error makes it invisible. Don't bail the whole walk on one unrecognized item, and don't anticipate every value shape. Either consume arbitrary value tokens (e.g. on `peek(Paren)` parse a `proc_macro2::Group`/`TokenTree`, in addition to the `= Lit` branch), or scan the attribute's token stream for a `default` ident directly. The `&'static str` requirement is self-imposed: clap's `Command::about`/`Subcommand` accept `impl Into<StyledStr>`, which `String` already satisfies, so the leak exists only to satisfy an overly-concrete parameter type. Change the builders to `about: impl Into<clap::builder::StyledStr>` (and `target_help: impl Into<StyledStr>`), then pass the owned `format!(...)` strings directly and delete `intern`. -- [ ] `apps/code-context-cli/src/cli.rs:138` — The added lifecycle-CLI tests `help_displays_all_lifecycle_commands` and `version_flag` are near-verbatim copies of the same tests in kanban-cli; as each tool CLI gains the identical lifecycle command set, this boilerplate is being copied per crate rather than shared. If a third+ tool CLI repeats this, lift a shared test helper into `swissarmyhammer-cli-completions` taking the binary name and expected lifecycle command list as parameters (e.g. `assert_lifecycle_help(bin, &cmds)` / `assert_version_flag(bin)`), and have each crate's test call it. Low priority — the differing binary/command contract makes it borderline, so leaving it as per-crate boilerplate is also defensible.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-cli-completions/src/lifecycle.rs:1` — `lifecycle_subcommands_use_supplied_about` is named for verifying the per-tool `about` strings but only asserts `get_name()` equals the literal subcommand name ("serve"/"init"/"deinit"/"doctor"). The about argument passed in is never read, so the test gives false confidence that about wiring is covered when it isn't. Assert on the about string the test claims to check, e.g. `assert_eq!(serve_subcommand("serve about").get_about().unwrap().to_string(), "serve about")`, or rename the test to reflect that it only checks subcommand names.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-cli-completions/src/lifecycle.rs:298` — The lifecycle subcommand builders take `about: &'static str`, which forces `standard_op_cli` to `Box::leak` every per-tool about string through `intern`. The `&'static str` requirement is self-imposed: clap's `Command::about`/`Subcommand` accept `impl Into<StyledStr>`, which `String` already satisfies, so the leak exists only to satisfy an overly-concrete parameter type. Change the builders to `about: impl Into<clap::builder::StyledStr>` (and `target_help: impl Into<StyledStr>`), then pass the owned `format!(...)` strings directly and delete `intern`. This removes the deliberate leak with no behavior change.
